---
title: "Danilo Freire"
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
![](assets/img/profile.jpg){.profile-photo fig-alt="Photo of Danilo Freire"}

::: {.social-icons}
<a href="mailto:danilo.freire@emory.edu" title="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i></a>
<a href="https://github.com/danilofreire" title="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lB1WKOYAAAAJ" title="Google Scholar"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i></a>
<a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4712-6810" title="ORCID"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i></a>
:::

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

I am a Visiting Assistant Professor in the [Department of Data and Decision Sciences](https://quantitative.emory.edu/) at [Emory University](https://www.emory.edu) and an Honorary Senior Fellow at the [University of Lincoln](https://www.lincoln.ac.uk/socialsciences/). I hold a PhD in Political Economy from [King's College London](https://www.kcl.ac.uk/political-economy), an MA in International Relations from the [Graduate Institute Geneva](https://www.graduateinstitute.ch/international-relations-political-science-presentation), and an MA in Political Science from the [University of Sao Paulo](https://dcp.fflch.usp.br/en). Before joining Emory, I was a Postdoctoral Research Associate at [Brown University](https://www.brown.edu/academics/political-theory-project/) and a Senior Lecturer at the [University of Lincoln](https://www.lincoln.ac.uk/socialsciences/).

My research focuses on the challenges developing countries face in solving collective action problems and reducing political violence. I am particularly interested in public service delivery, accountability, private governance, and state violence. Most of my work centres on Latin America, where I use various methods including survey and field experiments, machine learning, and administrative data analysis.

My research has appeared in the *American Journal of Political Science*, *British Journal of Political Science*, *Royal Society Open Science*, *Journal of Experimental Political Science*, *Latin American Research Review*, and *Research & Politics*. I am also an [EGAP Fellow](https://egap.org).

You can find my publications on the [Research](research.qmd) page, download my [CV](DaniloFreireCV.pdf), or browse my projects on [GitHub](https://github.com/danilofreire). If you have any questions or would like to discuss potential collaborations, please email me at [danilo.freire@emory.edu](mailto:danilo.freire@emory.edu). I am always keen to connect with fellow researchers and students.

:::

::::
